# src/billing.ts ‚Äî context summary

## What the file does
A 310-line domain module with 5 top-level functions covering billing logic. Its centerpiece is `generateInvoice` (lines 50‚Äì253, 204 lines), and it includes `calculateRefundPreview` (lines 40‚Äì44) which despite its name performs side effects (`saveRefundAudit`, `sendRefundEmail`). The file hard-codes the `"enterprise"` plan literal, reads wall-clock time in 7 places, and carries a prose-only authorization rule in a comment at line 39: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."*

## Files to read before editing
- **`src/billing.test.ts`** ‚Äî the only test file, and it's weak (one zero-assertion test, one with only weak matchers). Don't trust a green bar here.
- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** ‚Äî all share the `"enterprise"` literal. Any plan-shape change ripples to these.
- **`src/date.ts`** ‚Äî also flagged for Temporal Recklessness; likely candidate for / blocker to a shared clock abstraction.
- **Definitions of `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail`** ‚Äî referenced by `calculateRefundPreview`, needed to understand the hidden side-effect surface.
- **`src/routes/account/subscription.tsx`** and friends ‚Äî concept-alias drift around tenant/account/organisation, relevant if the edit touches who-owns-what.

## Risks the scan flagged on this file
| Severity | Charge | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| **high** | God Function | `generateInvoice` is 3.4√ó the 60-line domain threshold |
| medium | Temporal Recklessness | 5√ó `Date.now()` + 2√ó `new Date()` across lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273 |
| medium | False Identity | `calculateRefundPreview` reads pure but writes (audit + email) |
| medium | String Sprinkles | `"enterprise"` duplicated across 4 production files |
| medium | God File | 310 lines, just over the 300 threshold |
| medium | Logic in the Alibi | refund rule lives in a comment, not in code |
| low | Unfinished Business | 4 TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers (lines 183‚Äì308) |

## Practical implication for the edit
Before touching `generateInvoice` or refund logic: pin a clock, treat `calculateRefundPreview` as side-effecting, and assume the `"enterprise"` literal and the comment-rule at line 39 both need to move (or at minimum be honored) anywhere policy is changed. The tests will not catch regressions ‚Äî add assertions if you change behavior.
